title: Utility and expected decisions deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Utility and expected decisions; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Utility and expected decisions: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? The action with the largest expected monetary value is objectively rational for every stakeholder and ethical context.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Make outcome utilities, probabilities, stakeholders, constraints, and sensitivity explicit; expected utility supports a bounded choice rather than neutralizing values.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Make outcome utilities, probabilities, stakeholders, constraints, and sensitivity explicit; expected utility supports a bounded choice rather than neutralizing values.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

items.title: Utility and expected decisions: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read success probability, success utility, and failure utility for actions A and B. Print the higher expected-utility action and both expectations to two decimals; ties choose A.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.referenceSolution:
import sys

def solve(data: str) -> str:
    values = list(map(float, data.split()))
    pa, usa, ufa, pb, usb, ufb = values
    a = pa * usa + (1 - pa) * ufa
    b = pb * usb + (1 - pb) * ufb
    return f"{'A' if a >= b else 'B'} {a:.2f} {b:.2f}"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solve(sys.stdin.read()), end="")
answer.explanation: The calculation exposes both probabilities and utilities; it is a bounded arithmetic comparison, not a claim that the utilities are neutral.
